Scenarios.title: Explore AI delivery scenarios by business journey
Scenarios.summary: Each scenario connects business outcomes, phases, metrics, and reusable reference architectures.

intelligentCustomerOperations.title: Intelligent Customer Operations
intelligentCustomerOperations.summary: Reframe service operations around grounded copilots, policy-aware automation, and continuous quality loops.
intelligentCustomerOperations.businessOutcome: Reduce handling time while raising first-contact resolution across digital and voice channels.

phases.title: Observe and prioritize
phases.description: Map high-volume intents, exception paths, policy dependencies, and current leakage points across channels.

phases.title: Ground and automate
phases.description: Introduce retrieval-backed copilots, guided resolution playbooks, and event-driven follow-up actions.

phases.title: Measure and optimize
phases.description: Track containment quality, escalations, and sentiment recovery through weekly improvement loops.

regulatedRiskCaseOrchestration.title: Regulated Risk Case Orchestration
regulatedRiskCaseOrchestration.summary: Coordinate document review, analyst workbenches, and human approvals for high-trust financial workflows.
regulatedRiskCaseOrchestration.businessOutcome: Shorten risk review lead time without weakening traceability, approvals, or policy controls.

phases.title: Standardize evidence intake
phases.description: Normalize documents, transaction records, and reviewer notes into a consistent case timeline.

phases.title: Assist decision preparation
phases.description: Surface anomalies, pre-populate rationales, and align recommendations with internal control language.

phases.title: Close with lineage
phases.description: Persist every decision, override, and supporting artifact for downstream audit and model governance.

fieldServiceKnowledgeAcceleration.title: Field Service Knowledge Acceleration
fieldServiceKnowledgeAcceleration.summary: Equip technicians with multimodal guidance, parts intelligence, and job-completion capture in the flow of work.
fieldServiceKnowledgeAcceleration.businessOutcome: Lift first-time fix rates and compress onboarding time for newly trained field teams.

phases.title: Digitize tacit knowledge
phases.description: Capture expert procedures, parts dependencies, and exception handling into guided digital playbooks.

phases.title: Assist on site
phases.description: Blend asset history, troubleshooting trees, and visual recognition into a single field experience.

phases.title: Feed the loop
phases.description: Convert job outcomes and technician feedback into refreshed content and improved dispatch logic.

Architectures.title: Reference architectures for trusted AI delivery
Architectures.summary: Use reusable patterns to connect experience, orchestration, knowledge, controls, and execution layers.

agenticServiceMesh.title: Agentic Service Mesh
agenticServiceMesh.summary: A modular orchestration pattern for copilots and automations that separates policy, memory, routing, and execution.

layers.title: Experience layer
layers.description: Desktop, CRM, portal, and channel integrations surface task context, recommendations, and approvals.

layers.title: Agent orchestration layer
layers.description: Intent routing, prompt composition, tool calling, fallback logic, and supervisor controls live here.

layers.title: Knowledge and memory layer
layers.description: Document retrieval, semantic memory, conversation state, and policy references are versioned and governed.

layers.title: Execution layer
layers.description: Workflow engines and enterprise systems execute updates, case actions, notifications, and data writes.

governance: Separate prompt assets from policy assets for independent review.
Log every tool call, approval handoff, and generated rationale.
Maintain rollback controls for model and retrieval changes.
deploymentNotes: Start with a narrow domain and one channel before broadening tool access.
Use shared tracing identifiers across orchestration and downstream systems.
Treat confidence thresholds as business controls, not model-only settings.

knowledgeGovernanceFabric.title: Knowledge Governance Fabric
knowledgeGovernanceFabric.summary: A governed content pipeline for high-trust retrieval use cases where freshness, lineage, and approval matter.

layers.title: Source ingestion
layers.description: Policies, documents, tickets, and expert notes are normalized, classified, and version controlled on ingest.

layers.title: Curation layer
layers.description: Editorial workflows detect conflicts, missing metadata, and stale assets before publication to retrieval.

layers.title: Serving layer
layers.description: Search indexes, embeddings, and authorization-aware retrieval deliver context by persona and workflow state.

layers.title: Feedback loop
layers.description: Usage analytics, missing-answer reports, and reviewer overrides feed ongoing curation and retirement decisions.

governance: Attach source lineage and review status to every retrievable artifact.
Enforce access scopes before retrieval and before answer generation.
Measure retrieval quality with human review on sensitive use cases.
deploymentNotes: Define a publication SLA for high-volatility policy domains.
Separate draft, approved, and retired knowledge states in storage and UX.
Use business ownership for content freshness, not only platform ownership.

trustControlPlane.title: Trust Control Plane
trustControlPlane.summary: A cross-cutting control plane for evaluation, approval, policy enforcement, and operational risk management.

layers.title: Policy definition
layers.description: Business rules, escalation thresholds, and approval routes are declared as reusable controls.

layers.title: Runtime enforcement
layers.description: Requests are evaluated for access, confidence, risk class, and mandatory review before action execution.

layers.title: Evaluation services
layers.description: Offline and online evaluations score recommendation quality, hallucination risk, and drift indicators.

layers.title: Evidence store
layers.description: Decision packets, review outcomes, and audit artifacts are retained for governance and remediation.

governance: Promote controls independently from model or workflow changes.
Make exception handling explicit with owner, expiry, and rationale.
Use policy simulation before releasing controls to production.
deploymentNotes: Integrate with case systems early to preserve analyst trust.
Treat eval datasets as governed assets with periodic refresh cycles.
Give risk teams self-service visibility without requiring engineering changes.

edgeInferenceBackbone.title: Edge Inference Backbone
edgeInferenceBackbone.summary: A resilient pattern for field and plant environments where local assistive intelligence must tolerate connectivity gaps.

layers.title: Device experience
layers.description: Tablet, rugged device, and wearable surfaces provide guided tasks, image capture, and parts lookup.

layers.title: Local inference and cache
layers.description: Compact models and curated knowledge bundles support on-site assistance when bandwidth is constrained.

layers.title: Sync and telemetry
layers.description: Bi-directional sync handles work orders, asset updates, and deferred evidence uploads.

layers.title: Central learning loop
layers.description: Headquarters teams retrain, review field outcomes, and publish improved playbooks back to the edge.

governance: Bound local model scope to approved offline tasks.
Encrypt local caches and expire sensitive content automatically.
Separate operational telemetry from worker performance management data.
deploymentNotes: Design graceful degradation before optimizing peak online performance.
Version field playbooks to avoid ambiguous instructions across crews.
Instrument sync success and stale-content rates as first-class health metrics.

Insights.title: Short insights that help teams align operating choices
Insights.summary: Use practical perspectives to connect governance, experience design, knowledge systems, and productized delivery.

fromPrototypesToGovernedAiProducts.title: From Prototypes to Governed AI Products
fromPrototypesToGovernedAiProducts.summary: Why many pilots stall and what has to change in portfolio management, ownership, and delivery design.
keyPoints: Prototype success rarely predicts production adoption on its own.
Governance has to be embedded in product flow, not bolted on after launch.
Teams need reusable controls, shared telemetry, and clear asset ownership.

bodySections.title: The failure mode
bodySections.description: Many pilots prove a model can generate outputs, but they do not prove that the surrounding workflow can absorb those outputs safely and repeatedly.

bodySections.title: What scales
bodySections.description: The strongest programs industrialize evaluation, prompt and policy versioning, and business-ready review paths before multiplying use cases.

bodySections.title: What to do next
bodySections.description: Treat AI delivery as a product portfolio with clear service tiers, reusable components, and operating metrics that matter to business owners.

designingRetrievalForHighTrustWorkflows.title: Designing Retrieval for High-Trust Workflows
designingRetrievalForHighTrustWorkflows.summary: Retrieval quality depends as much on editorial and governance systems as it does on embeddings and chunking.
keyPoints: Freshness and lineage matter more as consequence increases.
Authorization and review state must travel with the content.
Missing-answer analytics are a product signal, not an edge case.

bodySections.title: Shift the question
bodySections.description: The real design problem is not only how to find similar text, but how to serve the right approved context to the right role at the right point in a workflow.

bodySections.title: Editorial systems are part of the architecture
bodySections.description: Without clear review, ownership, and retirement patterns, even strong retrieval infrastructure degrades into a confidence problem for end users.

bodySections.title: Measure usefulness, not only relevance
bodySections.description: Useful retrieval reduces back-and-forth, supports action completion, and exposes when the system cannot answer with confidence.

whyAgentExperienceNeedsJourneyDesign.title: Why Agent Experience Needs Journey Design
whyAgentExperienceNeedsJourneyDesign.summary: Copilots fail when they are inserted as side widgets instead of being designed around the job-to-be-done.
keyPoints: Assistive AI should reduce decisions, not create another interface to manage.
Journey mapping reveals where confidence and approvals really matter.
The best experiences combine automation with transparent next-best actions.

bodySections.title: Move from feature thinking to journey thinking
bodySections.description: Users do not want a chatbot feature; they want a shorter, clearer path to a completed outcome with fewer risky decisions.

bodySections.title: Design for confidence
bodySections.description: Grounding signals, source previews, and approval checkpoints make assistance easier to trust in real operating contexts.

bodySections.title: Design for handoff
bodySections.description: Human takeover, escalation, and case continuity should feel native, not like failure states.

Community.title: Connect with practitioners, labs, and working groups
Community.summary: Join community programs around reusable AI delivery patterns, governance, and architecture practices.

buildersRoundtableQ3.title: Builders Roundtable Q3
buildersRoundtableQ3.summary: A peer forum for platform, architecture, and product leaders aligning on reusable enterprise AI delivery patterns.

agenda.title: Reusable platform patterns
agenda.description: Share delivery accelerators, control models, and rollout sequencing across teams.

agenda.title: Assurance at scale
agenda.description: Compare evaluation practices, release gates, and business-facing evidence models.

agenda.title: Working session
agenda.description: Map common blockers and publish a short pattern pack after the event.

fieldAiPractitionerLab.title: Field AI Practitioner Lab
fieldAiPractitionerLab.summary: A practical program focused on frontline and field-service AI patterns, from knowledge capture to edge delivery.
fieldAiPractitionerLab.audience: Operations leads and field transformation teams

agenda.title: Workflow teardown
agenda.description: Decompose current field journeys into assistive moments, controls, and measurable outcomes.

agenda.title: Architecture clinic
agenda.description: Review offline-first design choices, sync boundaries, and device telemetry needs.

agenda.title: Capability roadmap
agenda.description: Translate lab findings into a staged delivery roadmap with readiness checkpoints.

governedRagWorkingGroup.title: Governed RAG Working Group
governedRagWorkingGroup.summary: A recurring collaboration stream for teams building retrieval-heavy experiences in regulated or high-trust domains.
governedRagWorkingGroup.audience: Knowledge platform teams and governance leads

agenda.title: Pattern review
agenda.description: Discuss content lifecycle, authorization-aware retrieval, and line-of-defense responsibilities.

agenda.title: Metrics exchange
agenda.description: Benchmark freshness, missing-answer rates, and reviewer intervention across implementations.

agenda.title: Artifact share-out
agenda.description: Publish templates, governance checklists, and architecture reference notes for members.
